compiled_state.title: Add Integration Setup section to INSTALL.md (Jira, Figma, Confluence) preserving earlier content; clear RED marker
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## Purpose
Add an `## Integration Setup` section to INSTALL.md with Jira, Figma, and Confluence subsections per the story's done definitions. All earlier INSTALL.md content must be preserved unchanged.

## File Impact
- INSTALL.md — EDIT (add Integration Setup section after existing sections)
- .test-index — EDIT (remove [test_install_integration_setup] marker)

## Implementation Steps

1. Open `INSTALL.md`. Locate a good insertion point — after the `## Getting Started with /dso:onboarding` section is natural.

2. Add the following `## Integration Setup` section (adapt wording as needed; do not alter earlier sections):

   ## Integration Setup

   Some DSO skills integrate with external tools. Each integration is optional and configured via environment variables or the DSO config file. Skip any integration you don't use.

   ### Jira

   DSO's ticket system can sync to Jira issues. To enable, set:

   - `JIRA_URL` — your Jira base URL (e.g., `https://your-org.atlassian.net`)
   - `JIRA_USER` — the email address of the Atlassian account used for API access
   - `JIRA_API_TOKEN` — an Atlassian API token

   Create an API token at: https://support.atlassian.com/atlassian-account/docs/manage-api-tokens-for-your-atlassian-account/

   ### Figma

   DSO's design collaboration features can pull Figma designs into your implementation manifests. To enable, set a Figma personal access token via `FIGMA_PAT` (or the equivalent DSO config key `design.figma_pat`).

   Create a personal access token at: https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/8085703771159-Manage-personal-access-tokens

   ### Confluence

   Confluence integration is planned but not yet available — no setup steps at this time.

3. Remove the `[test_install_integration_setup]` marker from `.test-index`.
4. Run `bash tests/test-install-integration-setup.sh` and confirm exit 0.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci
- [ ] INSTALL.md contains Integration Setup section
  Verify: grep -F '## Integration Setup' INSTALL.md
- [ ] Contains Jira subsection with Atlassian API token guide URL
  Verify: grep -F 'support.atlassian.com/atlassian-account/docs/manage-api-tokens' INSTALL.md
- [ ] Contains Figma subsection with PAT guide URL
  Verify: grep -F 'help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/8085703771159' INSTALL.md
- [ ] Contains Confluence placeholder
  Verify: grep -F '### Confluence' INSTALL.md
- [ ] Earlier content preserved
  Verify: grep -qF '/plugin marketplace add navapbc/digital-service-orchestra' INSTALL.md && grep -qF '## Prerequisites' INSTALL.md
- [ ] Integration Setup test passes
  Verify: bash tests/test-install-integration-setup.sh
- [ ] RED marker removed
  Verify: ! grep -qF '[test_install_integration_setup]' .test-index

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 1/6: Read INSTALL.md and test file. Adding Integration Setup section to INSTALL.md.
comments.author: Test

Will commit both changes to make HEAD match intended state.
comments.author: Test

RED marker removal from .test-index is in working tree (Edit succeeded) but suite-engine.sh restores from HEAD after each test run. This is expected — changes become permanent at commit time.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 4/6: All 7 acceptance criteria verified. INSTALL.md has Integration Setup section with Jira/Figma/Confluence subsections. test-install-integration-setup.sh passes 18/18. RED marker removed from .test-index (stable - background suite-engine processes from this worktree have finished).
